message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker with SEC-4.1 quota and supply-chain provenance progress

Update beads database to reflect:
- SEC-4.1 (bd-b1d7o) resource quota engine implementation progress
- Supply-chain provenance lockfile work status updates
- Issue dependency graph updates for SEC workstream beads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -73,7 +73,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2ki","title":"Tooling: Compatibility rewrite map + extc contract","description":"# Goal
Define the **compatibility contract** that maps legacy Node/Bun imports to PiJS shims so **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json` run unmodified** (no manual source edits).

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions** in extc rewrites.
- Rewrites must be defined solely in terms of import specifiers and generic, semantics-preserving code patterns.
- If the sample reveals a gap, fix it by adding a **general rule + tests**, not by branching on extension id.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Canonical rewrite map for imports + globals.
- Explicitly forbidden APIs with clear diagnostics.
- Extc input/output contract: ESM output, shim injection rules, side-effect policy.
- Compatibility matrix of supported APIs and required capabilities.
- Source map contract for accurate error locations.

# Canonical rewrite / resolution rules
## Import specifiers
Extc must ensure every import specifier is resolvable inside PiJS without Node/Bun.

Recommended canonicalization:

### A) Node builtins (`node:*`)
Rewrite `node:*` builtins to an internal namespace that PiJS provides (so bundlers don’t externalize them):
- `node:fs`              -> `pi:node/fs`
- `node:fs/promises`     -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `node:path`            -> `pi:node/path`
- `node:os`              -> `pi:node/os`
- `node:url`             -> `pi:node/url`
- `node:crypto`          -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `node:child_process`   -> `pi:node/child_process` (or rewrite usage to `pi.exec` when semantics preserved)
- `node:module`          -> `pi:node/module`

### B) Bare builtins (`'fs'`, `'path'`, ...)
Many real-world deps import builtins without the `node:` prefix. Treat these identically:
- `fs`            -> `pi:node/fs`
- `fs/promises`   -> `pi:node/fs_promises`
- `path`          -> `pi:node/path`
- `os`            -> `pi:node/os`
- `url`           -> `pi:node/url`
- `crypto`        -> `pi:node/crypto`
- `child_process` -> `pi:node/child_process`
- `module`        -> `pi:node/module`

## Global polyfills (injection)
Extc may inject an idempotent prelude import at the bundle entrypoint:
- `import 'pi:polyfills/node_globals'` (installs `process`, `Buffer`, etc.)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/fetch'` (if needed)
- `import 'pi:polyfills/webassembly'` (assume QuickJS has no wasm; installs PiWasm bridge)

Injection must be:
- deterministic (stable ordering)
- sourcemap-correct
- versioned (shim_version included in artifact hash)

# Forbidden / flagged APIs
- The contract must list:
  - **Forbidden** (hard error): APIs that bypass capability policy or escape sandbox.
  - **Flagged** (warning w/ evidence): risky constructs (dynamic eval, unbounded recursion, etc.).

Notes:
- The pinned sample includes `new Function(...)` for loading a bundled script; do not forbid it. Prefer “flag + log” unless we can prove safe.

# Compatibility matrix (must be sample-driven)
- The matrix must enumerate the exact Node builtins/globals required by the pinned sample set and define:
  - supported functions
  - sync vs async semantics
  - error mapping expectations
  - capability requirements + scopes

Implementation beads:
- Node core shims: bd-3d0
- node:child_process subset: bd-2sr
- WebAssembly bridge (PiWasm): bd-1ry

# Tests
- Unit transform fixtures for common imports and injection.
- Negative tests for forbidden APIs with exact messages.
- E2E harness verifies rewritten bundles run 16/16 with actionable failures.

# Dependencies
- Must align with the PiJS runtime contract + event loop semantics (bd-123) and hostcall ABI (bd-37z).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:59.736950487Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:21:50.124812910Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:21:50.124743731Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added §2A Extc Compatibility Contract to EXTENSIONS.md with canonical rewrite rules, global polyfill injection, forbidden/flagged APIs, compatibility matrix, and sourcemap contract","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:05.067354913Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ki","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:05.533406188Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qd","title":"Define per-extension scenario suite for capture","description":"Background:
- Each extension may expose tools, slash commands, and event hooks; we must test them consistently.

Steps:
- For each sampled extension, enumerate available features (tools/commands/events).
- Define minimal smoke scenarios + edge cases (inputs, expected outputs).
- Document any required configuration or secrets (mocked).

Output:
- A scenario specification mapped to each extension in the sample manifest.

Acceptance:
- Every extension has at least one scenario per supported feature category.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:28.650419393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:47:33.619339695Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:47:33.619279633Z","close_reason":"Completed: added extension capture scenario suite spec","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qd","depends_on_id":"bd-ic9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:34:39.934693450Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3on","title":"Implement legacy capture pipeline (run + record outputs)","description":"Background:
- We need golden fixtures from legacy pi for each scenario.

Steps:
- Build a capture runner that executes scenarios and records raw outputs (stdout/stderr, JSON results).
- Record metadata: pi-mono commit, extension version, environment snapshot.
- Ensure the runner can be re-run deterministically.
- Emit structured capture logs per the logging spec for traceability.

Acceptance:
- For a small pilot subset, the capture output is repeatable and complete.
- Logs enable debugging without rerunning capture.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:22:37.848853767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T09:36:16.926264391Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T09:36:16.926205121Z","close_reason":"Added pi_legacy_capture runner (print/json + mock OpenAI) capturing pilot scenarios scn-permission-gate-001 + scn-plan-mode-002 deterministically","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3on","depends_on_id":"bd-2qd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:35:06.569454464Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3on","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:58:45.777576081Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3on","depends_on_id":"bd-7l6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:34:48.585575300Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3on","depends_on_id":"bd-u4r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:34:59.235697095Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3bs","title":"Tooling: Compatibility scanner + evidence ledger","description":"# Goal
Ship a scanner that flags Node/Bun-only APIs and produces an **evidence ledger** for compatibility decisions.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Static scan for unsupported imports/APIs, including:
  - `node:*` builtins
  - bare builtin specifiers (`fs`, `path`, `child_process`, ...)
  - common risky constructs (e.g. `new Function`, `eval`) as *flagged* (unless explicitly forbidden)
- Output: list of required capabilities and reasons (line-level evidence).
- Actionable remediation hints.
- Stable, deterministic output ordering for diffability.

# Alien-Artifact Angle
- Evidence ledger is human-auditable and deterministic.
- Every rejection points to exact source locations.

# Tests
- Unit fixtures for scan results and ordering.
- Fixture proving the pinned sample set yields a stable ledger.
- E2E harness validates ledger presence in logs.

# Dependencies
- Depends on the rewrite contract (bd-2ki) so the scanner’s findings match what extc can actually rewrite.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:23:08.773207010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:18:17.094267130Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:18:17.094199564Z","close_reason":"Implemented compat scanner + compat ledger logging + tests (snapshots + e2e); gates green","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bs","depends_on_id":"bd-2ki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:05.992165867Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-576","title":"Align extension protocol schema + Rust types with legacy","description":"Background:

Steps:
- Compare docs/schema/extension_protocol.json + docs/wit/extension.wit to legacy behavior.

Acceptance:

Updated validation + register parsing test. Checks run: cargo check/clippy/fmt still failing due to existing http/asupersync and formatting issues unrelated to this bead.","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:57:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-x85o","title":"Interactive: /tree full navigator UI + branch summarization","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `/tree` semantics and interactive UI as described in `legacy .../docs/tree.md`.

# Required UI/Controls (legacy)
- Display a depth-first tree list with indentation.
- Mark the current leaf as `← active`.
- Controls:
  - Up/Down: move selection
  - Enter: select
  - Escape/Ctrl+C: cancel
  - Ctrl+U: toggle user messages only
  - Ctrl+O: toggle show-all (include custom/label entries)

# Selection Behavior (legacy)
- Selecting a **user message** or **custom message**:
  1) leaf set to parent of selected node (or null if root)
  2) message text placed in editor for re-submission
- Selecting a **non-user message**:
  1) leaf set to selected node
  2) editor remains empty

# Branch Summarization (legacy)
Before switching branches, user chooses:
1) No summary
2) Summarize (default prompt)
3) Summarize with custom prompt

Summarize the abandoned path from old leaf back to common ancestor (or stop at compaction node).
Store result as a branch_summary entry and attach to the new leaf.

# Implementation Notes
- Current Rust `/tree` is a non-interactive listing + manual switch; this task replaces that with a selector.
- Reuse existing session tree operations where possible (`Session::list_leaves`, `navigate_to`, `append_branch_summary`).
- Ensure agent context is updated after switching leaf.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/tree` UX and semantics match legacy docs.
- [ ] Optional summary is created/stored correctly.
- [ ] Selecting a user message pre-fills the editor.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:48.715601393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:32.326869879Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:19:32.326803556Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-x85o","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:48.721842373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-x85o","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:42:35.264087799Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uj","title":"Unit tests: WASM hostcalls + sandbox boundaries","description":"Background:
- WASM hostcalls must enforce capabilities and sandboxing rigorously.

Steps:
- Unit tests for each hostcall (read/write/exec/http/env) with allow/deny cases.
- Ensure errors propagate with correct codes/messages.
- Validate resource cleanup and cancellation behavior.

Logging requirements:
- Tests assert hostcall logs include extension id, capability, and decision.

Acceptance:
- Hostcall behavior matches policy and is stable under error conditions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:46:20.154969636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:54.282252793Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uj","depends_on_id":"bd-2hr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:54.282223929Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uj","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:46.851853811Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uj","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:48:50.949777504Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uj","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:49:07.235468737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -239,7 +239,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-gor5","title":"Workstream: Docs/spec drift fix (README/PLAN/FEATURE_PARITY)","description":"# Goal
Bring repo “source-of-truth” documents back into alignment with the *actual* codebase state.

This workstream exists because several key docs currently describe historical/incomplete states (e.g., tokio/reqwest migration still “ongoing”), while the codebase is already on `asupersync` and has new tests/conformance coverage that isn’t reflected.

# Why this matters
- Engineers (and agents) use these docs to decide what’s “left” → drift causes duplicate work and wrong prioritization.
- `FEATURE_PARITY.md` is intended to be authoritative; if it’s stale, we lose our parity tracking backbone.
- README is the first thing a new contributor reads; it must not mislead.

# Scope
1) Update README to reflect current runtime/stack choices and remove outdated “tokio still handles runtime” claims.
2) Update `PLAN_TO_COMPLETE_PORT.md` to reflect what’s already complete and explicitly defer to Beads for remaining work.
3) Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` to reflect:
   - current provider streaming implementation details (no reqwest references if none exist)
   - correct bead IDs for remaining gaps (e.g., themes)
   - current conformance/test coverage status (session/provider/CLI coverage now exists)
4) Update `AGENTS.md` “current status” sections that mention tokio migration as ongoing, if still present.

# Output / Definition of Done
- README, PLAN, and FEATURE_PARITY tell the same story about what’s done vs remaining.
- Any “remaining work” listed in docs links to the appropriate bead(s).
- No extension-runtime deep work is done here (extensions have separate workstreams); this is documentation accuracy.

# Notes
This is intentionally structured as multiple small child tasks so we can land doc fixes incrementally without one huge mega-diff.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:03:01.025855880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:03:01.038531779Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gor5","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T22:03:01.038475594Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28uy","title":"Interactive: /copy copies last assistant message to clipboard","description":"# Goal
Implement legacy `/copy` behavior: copy last assistant message text to the system clipboard.

# Required Behavior
- If there is no assistant message yet: show an error (“No agent messages to copy yet.” or equivalent).
- On success: show a short status (“Copied …”).
- On failure: show an actionable error (clipboard unavailable).

# Implementation Notes
- Prefer a cross-platform clipboard crate with minimal optional dependencies.
- Avoid feature-gating the entire command away; at minimum provide a runtime fallback that prints the text or writes it to a temp file with a clear message.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/copy` works out-of-the-box on macOS/Linux/Windows where clipboard is available.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:01.932575094Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:01.938838364Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28uy","depends_on_id":"bd-1u0c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:43:01.938806395Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-lqec","title":"Interactive: /fork creates new session file from current branch","description":"# Goal
Bring `/fork` to legacy semantics: create a **new session file** from the current branch and switch to it.

# Legacy Semantics (from `legacy .../docs/tree.md`)
- `/fork` extracts the current branch path into a **new session file**.
- `/tree` changes leaf within the same session file.

# Required Behavior
- When invoked, `/fork` should:
  - choose a fork point (current leaf by default, or selected user message if UI requests)
  - create a new session JSONL with entries representing the selected path
  - set the new session’s leaf appropriately
  - switch the app to the new session

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] After `/fork`, subsequent messages append to the new session file.
- [ ] Original session remains unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T19:41:58.151807778Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:52:06.410406596Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T22:52:06.410341445Z","close_reason":"Completed: /fork creates new session file + prefill editor","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lqec","depends_on_id":"bd-14cc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T23:38:21Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-lqec","depends_on_id":"bd-gze","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T23:38:21Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1pb","title":"Perf: Baseline + optimization plan (alien-grade rigor)","description":"# Goal
Establish a **profile-driven optimization loop** for the PiJS runtime and connectors with reproducible outputs.

# Method (Extreme Optimization)
1) Baseline: `hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10` on key extension workloads.
2) Profile: `cargo flamegraph` for CPU, heaptrack for allocations.
3) Prove: golden outputs + isomorphism checklist for every change.
4) Implement: only opportunities with score ≥ 2.0.
5) Verify: sha256 of golden outputs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Benchmark scripts for representative extensions.
- Opportunity matrix with impact/confidence/effort.
- Initial flamegraph and top hotspots.
- Log artifacts per run (JSONL + summary table).

# Output
- Short performance report tied to `bd-btq` success criteria.","notes":"Progress (2026-02-03): Added Criterion microbench group ext_js_runtime for QuickJsRuntime (cold start, warm eval, run_pending_jobs empty) in benches/extensions.rs. Documented baseline numbers + repeatable loop + opportunity matrix template in BENCHMARKS.md. Gates green: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test. Remaining: hyperfine-style end-to-end extension workloads; initial flamegraph; opportunity matrix/hotspot list with evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:23:45.780267200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T22:21:55.152400910Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:26.236917347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:25.317622080Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:25.777714572Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2hr","title":"Spec: Unified JS+WASM capability model","description":"# Goal
Define a **single, coherent capability model** that applies equally to JS (PiJS) and WASM extensions, so security policy, logs, and tooling are identical across runtimes.

# Why
- Avoid divergent permission semantics between JS and WASM.
- Enable shared tooling (manifests, scanners, audit logs) and a consistent user mental model.
- JS-tier may execute wasm via PiWasm bridge (bd-1ry); capability semantics must remain identical.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Capability taxonomy: common names, scopes, and semantics across JS/WASM.
- Hostcall mapping: how JS `pi.*` calls and WASM hostcalls map to the same capabilities.
- Manifest alignment: how a single manifest expresses capabilities for both runtimes.
- Logging schema alignment: identical fields and redaction rules.

# Constraints
- Must compose with `bd-37z` ABI.
- Must be compatible with WASM host (`bd-nom`) and JS runtime (`bd-123`).

# Tests This Enables
- Cross-runtime capability conformance tests.
- E2E security suite verifying identical enforcement paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:44.048778878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T20:22:35.300434438Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:22:35.300367473Z","close_reason":"Unified JS+WASM capability model spec added","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hr","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:52.819769328Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hr","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:52.320774486Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39u","title":"Unit tests: JS runtime + shims + event loop ordering","description":"Background:
- JS compatibility requires correct promise/microtask ordering and shim behavior.
- This bead is the **ordering/invariants test suite** for bd-123 + bd-1f5.

Steps:
- Unit/property tests for the PiJS event loop ordering:
  - macrotask vs microtask fixpoint draining
  - timer tie-breaking stability
  - hostcall completion enqueue order stability
- Tests for cancellation + timeout behavior across the promise bridge.
- Trace-log assertions for ordering and promise resolution sequence (diffable).

Acceptance:
- Ordering invariants from bd-123 hold and are reproducible under deterministic harness control.

 {"id":"bd-2sr","title":"Impl: node:child_process subset (spawn/kill + streams) for full sample","description":"# Goal
Implement the **minimum `node:child_process` subset** required for **all 16 extensions in `docs/extension-sample.json`** to run **unmodified** under the PiJS JS tier.

This bead exists because `child_process` semantics (spawn + events/streams) are the hardest Node surface area in the sample set.

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions**: implementation must not branch on extension id/name.
- The shim must be a generic `pi:node/child_process` module whose behavior depends only on inputs + policy/capabilities.
- If a sample exposes a semantic gap, fix it by tightening the generic contract + tests, not by adding special cases.

# Scope (must match pinned-sample usage)
## API
- `spawn(command, args?, options?)` returning a `ChildProcess`-like object.

## Options to accept (best-effort)
Pinned sample uses:
- `cwd`
- `detached: true|false`
- `shell: false` (explicit)
- `stdio: ['ignore'|'pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe']`

The shim must accept these options and behave compatibly. Unsupported options must fail fast with an actionable error (not silent ignore).

## ChildProcess object surface
Pinned sample requires:
- `.pid` (number)
- `.killed` (boolean)
- `.kill(signal?)`
- `.on('close', (code) => ...)`
- `.on('error', (err) => ...)`
- `.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => ...)`
- `.stderr.on('data', (chunk) => ...)`

### Stream chunk semantics
- `chunk` must support `toString()` and roundtrip the emitted bytes.

## Non-goals
- Full Node streams implementation
- `fork`/`execFile`/`execSync`, IPC channels

# Design constraints
- **Capability gated**: all process execution must go through the connector dispatcher (bd-h04) with `exec` capability.
- **Process-tree cleanup**: integrate with Pi’s existing process-tree cleanup semantics.
- **Backpressure + size limits**: prevent unbounded memory from stdout/stderr capture.
- **Deterministic test mode**: allow harness to run with mocked process execution / recorded outputs.

# Related compatibility
- `process.kill(pid, signal?)` is part of the Node globals shim (bd-3d0). For safety, it must be restricted to pids created by this `spawn` implementation or denied by policy.

# Compatibility strategy (must support both paths)
- Path A (preferred): provide a real shim that satisfies the Node API surface the bundled deps call.
- Path B (compiler assist): extc rewrite can optionally map `spawn(...)` patterns to `await pi.exec(...)` when semantics are provably preserved (generic pattern-based rewrite only).

# Acceptance (hard)
- Sample extensions that transitively depend on `child_process` run **without manual source edits**.
- Policy denial produces an actionable error and a structured audit log entry.
- No extension-id special-casing (enforced by review/tests).

# Tests (required)
- Unit tests: spawn lifecycle, stdout/stderr data delivery, events ordering, kill semantics, `.killed` behavior.
- Negative tests: denied `exec` capability.
- Integration tests in QuickJS exercising the pinned-sample spawn patterns.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:36:41.820928089Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:11:18.791358405Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2sr","depends_on_id":"bd-1f5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:48.962447519Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2sr","depends_on_id":"bd-1jn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:51.058682771Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2sr","depends_on_id":"bd-2ki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:50.522051609Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2sr","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:50.009351048Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2sr","depends_on_id":"bd-3d0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:51.570343185Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2sr","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:49.481977426Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
